title: centaurea × valentina nothovar. aemiliae
intro: Centaurea × valentina nothovar. aemiliae, commonly known as Cornflower, is a hybrid plant species within the aster family, derived from the cross between Centaurea cyanus and Centaurea valentina. Traditionally, the Cornflower plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cornflower include soothing skin irritations, reducing anxiety and stress, and supporting respiratory health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Cornflower is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
